questions: Which tests write to the live .qor/gates tree, and through which missed-redirect shape?
Are the tracked fixture dirs consumed by any ledger entry, module, or test read?
Is conftest-sweep widening a safe backstop?

findings.summary: test_gate_chain_provenance.py uses doppelganger imports (sys.path.insert + top-level import); patches never reach the canonical writer; test03/test04/test06 write live, test06 rewrites tracked plan.provenance with a tmp-keyed hmac
test_qor_ideate_writes_gate_artifact.py patches only the resolver constant; writes land via validate_gate_artifact.GATES_DIR into live ideate-e2e/ideate-prov
test_skill_active_env.py sets QORLOGIC_PROJECT_DIR (host install dirs) instead of QOR_ROOT and patches no GATES_DIR; live audit write + audit_history row per run
sid-string lookalikes verified non-offenders (synthetic dirs, pre-write raises, or conftest-swept sids)
five tracked pollution dirs have zero consumers; safe to git rm
conftest sweep widening unsafe (would match tracked historical session dirs); decline issue item 2
